pattern: Use a 2D prefix sum to evaluate every submatrix anchored at (0,0) in O(1) per query.
hints: Every valid submatrix must include cell (0,0); the number of such submatrices is determined by all (r,c) pairs where the prefix sum up to (r,c) is at most k.
Build a 2D prefix sum array so prefix[r][c] holds the sum of the rectangle from (0,0) to (r,c) in O(mn) time.
Iterate over every cell (r,c); if prefix[r][c] <= k, increment the answer; the prefix sum itself guarantees the top-left element is included.

edge_inputs.description: top-left zero but submatrix sum grows quickly

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 10) for _ in range(size)] for _ in range(size)]
    k = rng.randint(100, 5000)
    return [grid, k]
